100 Years Ago Today, This is What Was Happening ...

On April 29, 1926, the League of Nations continued its efforts to promote global peace, with discussions focusing on disarmament and international cooperation. In the United States, the Senate debated the controversial McNary-Haugen Bill, aimed at stabilizing agricultural prices, amidst growing tensions between farmers and urban interests. Meanwhile, in Germany, the government faced mounting pressure as economic instability persisted, leading to concerns about the potential resurgence of extremist movements.
